findings.issue: isSafeReturnTo('') returns true because new URL('', window.location.origin).origin equals window.location.origin. The test at AppRouter.test.tsx:377 explicitly asserts this. While the only caller (OAuthCallbackPage) guards against empty strings via `searchParams.get('return_to') || '/nodes'` (empty string is falsy, so the fallback kicks in), the exported function's contract is misleading: isSafeReturnTo('') === true implies an empty string is a safe redirect target, but window.location.assign('') reloads the current page (the OAuth callback URL), which could cause an infinite redirect loop if any future caller passes the raw value without the || fallback.
findings.remediation: Add an early return for empty/whitespace-only strings: if (!url.trim()) return false; or return parsed.origin === window.location.origin && parsed.pathname !== '/' when the input is empty, or simply document that callers must guard with ||.

findings.issue: In InvitationCompletePage.test.tsx, the 'shows error when stored verifier is missing' test (line 81) and the 'shows error when invitation token is missing' test (line 98) both set up vi.mocked(redeemOAuth).mockResolvedValue({...}), but the early-return branches in InvitationCompletePage.tsx (missing verifier at line 45, missing token at line 54) prevent redeemOAuth from ever being called. Neither test asserts expect(redeemOAuth).not.toHaveBeenCalled(). While the tests would still fail if the code called redeemOAuth before the checks AND showed the wrong UI (because the mocked acceptInvitation would proceed to setSuccess), a regression that calls redeemOAuth before the checks but still shows the error would waste a network request undetected.
findings.remediation: Add expect(redeemOAuth).not.toHaveBeenCalled() to both tests to explicitly assert the early-return branches skip the network call.
